messages.text: Requested the next high-value non-Amazon scrape and approved proceeding.

Claimed evelynbobbie_com, read the scrape rules and triage context, inspected the existing adapter/output, and found that the public Yotpo v3 store endpoint required perPage=100 rather than per_page=100.

Updated scrape_evelynbobbie_reviews.py to use Shopify products, sitemap/lead URL discovery, public Yotpo product endpoints, and the public Yotpo v3 store review feed. Added stop-on-429/captcha/WAF behavior, deterministic size and measurement parsing from review text/provider fields/productVariants, customer review image source fields, and product exclusion accounting for gift cards and accessories.
Ran the full scrape with FWM_DATA_DIR=/Users/briannasinger/Projects/FWM_Data and --request-delay-seconds 0.02. The run completed without rate-limit or challenge behavior.

Final metrics: 27 products discovered and scanned; 3 excluded products; 1,174 review pages scanned; 27,300 store-feed reviews observed; 2 store-feed media reviews observed; 2 rows written; 2 distinct images; 2 distinct product URLs; 1 row with ordered size; 1 row with deterministic measurements; 1 Supabase-qualified row; no errors.

Verified the script with python3 -m py_compile and checked CSV/summary consistency. Updated data-pipelines/non-amazon/docs/evelynbobbie_scrape_2026-05-11.md with the adapter, command, coverage, output metrics, and note that the triage media hint appears to be catalog/page media rather than public customer review media.
Requested updates to git, S3, and the transcripts table.

Synced the FWM_Data root to s3://fwm-scraping-data-briannasinger using the default AWS profile after the configured fwm profile was unavailable. The sync uploaded the Evelyn Bobbie CSV and summary JSON.
